README.md
TEST_PLAN_M1.md
tests
# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite

Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).

Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.

---

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

---

## Running Tests

### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)

```bash
npx playwright test
```

### By tier

```bash
npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
```

### Specific spec files

```bash
# M1 debug dashboard
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js

# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js

# Slot system / capacity
npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js

# Talk handshaking
npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js

# Fallback logic
npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
```

### With UI (for debugging)

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

---

## Configuration

| Setting | Default | Override |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |

To run against a local or staging deployment:

```bash
BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
```

---

## Test Structure

```
tests/
├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
├── smoke/
│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
├── regression/
│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
│   ├── character-facing.spec.js        # FRG-244: facing toward objects + characters during talk
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5173)
│   ├── walk-animation.spec.js          # FRG-204: walk animation stutter/freeze fix
│   │                                   #   moveTo resets walk state, large-delta frame accumulation,
│   │                                   #   full 4-frame cycle, walkTimer remainder invariant
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5174)
│   ├── contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js  # FRG-243: contextual anim per object type,
│   │                                   #   spriteKey/interactAnim during+after interaction, live sim,
│   │                                   #   all 9 interaction sprites (runs against localhost:3000)
│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types

USERMESSAGE #18
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-245
     4→ * Character-to-character collision avoidance.
     5→ *
     6→ * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5175) which serves
     7→ * the feature/frg-245-character-collision branch.
     8→ *
     9→ * The page does NOT expose window._gameEngine by default, so we intercept
    10→ * index.html via page.route() and inject the exposure before the script executes.
    11→ *
    12→ * Test layout:
    13→ *   1. No tile sharing — no two characters occupy the same tile when idle/interacting
    14→ *   2. _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles and preserves goal exemption
    15→ *   3. Retry and deadlock escape — _retryTimer counts down, _deadlockEscape fires at 3 failures
    16→ *   4. _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles when alternatives exist
    17→ *   5. Live sim stability — no permanently stuck characters over 120s of sim time
    18→ */
    19→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    20→
    21→const LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5175';
    22→const MAP_COLS = 16; // from tilemap.js — width of tile grid
    23→
    24→// ── helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25→
    26→/**
    27→ * Intercept index.html and inject window._gameEngine = engine so tests can
    28→ * call page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.*).
    29→ */
    30→async function injectEngineExposure(page) {
    31→  await page.route('**/', async (route) => {
    32→    const response = await route.fetch();
    33→    let body = await response.text();
    34→    body = body.replace(
    35→      '// ── Initial state ──',
    36→      '// ── Initial state (QA injection) ──
window._gameEngine = engine;'
    37→    );
    38→    await route.fulfill({ response, body });
    39→  });
    40→}
    41→
    42→/**
    43→ * Click start overlay (if visible) and optionally boost timeScale.
    44→ */
    45→async function startEngine(page, timeScale = 1) {
    46→  const overlay = page.locator('#start-overlay');
    47→  if (await overlay.isVisible()) {
    48→    await overlay.click();
    49→  }
    50→  if (timeScale !== 1) {
    51→    await page.evaluate((scale) => {
    52→      if (window._gameEngine) window._gameEngine.timeScale = scale;
    53→    }, timeScale);
    54→  }
    55→}
    56→
    57→/**
    58→ * Poll engine snapshot until predicate returns truthy or timeout.
    59→ */
    60→async function pollSnapshot(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 45_000, intervalMs = 300) {
    61→  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
    62→  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
    63→    const snap = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.snapshot());
    64→    if (predicate(snap)) return snap;
    65→    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);
    66→  }
    67→  throw new Error(`pollSnapshot timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);
    68→}
    69→
    70→// ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    71→
    72→test.describe('FRG-245: Character Collision Avoidance', () => {
    73→
    74→  // ── TEST 1: No tile sharing during idle/interacting ────────────────────────
    75→
    76→  test('no two characters share a tile when idle or interacting over 60s', async ({ page }) => {
    77→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    78→
    79→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
    80→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
    81→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
    82→    await startEngine(page, 20); // 20x speed so 60 sim-seconds = ~3 real seconds
    83→
    84→    const violations = [];
    85→    const POLL_COUNT = 30;
    86→    const POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 300; // ~9 real seconds at 20x = 180 sim-seconds
    87→
    88→    for (let i = 0; i < POLL_COUNT; i++) {
    89→      await page.waitForTimeout(POLL_INTERVAL_MS);
    90→
    91→      const charPositions = await page.evaluate(() =>
    92→        window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({
    93→          id: c.id,
    94→          tx: Math.round(c.tx),
    95→          ty: Math.round(c.ty),
    96→          action: c.action,
    97→        }))
    98→      );
    99→
   100→      // Only check tile sharing when characters are in a stable state (idle/interacting)
   101→      const stable = charPositions.filter(c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'interacting');
   102→
   103→      for (let a = 0; a < stable.length; a++) {
   104→        for (let b = a + 1; b < stable.length; b++) {
   105→          if (stable[a].tx === stable[b].tx && stable[a].ty === stable[b].ty) {
   106→            violations.push({
   107→              poll: i,
   108→              charA: stable[a].id,
   109→              charB: stable[b].id,
   110→              tile: `(${stable[a].tx},${stable[a].ty})`,
   111→              actions: `${stable[a].action}/${stable[b].action}`,
   112→            });
   113→          }
   114→        }
   115→      }
   116→    }
   117→
   118→    if (violations.length > 0) {
   119→      console.error('Tile-sharing violations:', JSON.stringify(violations, null, 2));
   120→    }
   121→
   122→    expect(violations.length,
   123→      `Characters shared tiles on ${violations.length} poll(s): ` +
   124→      violations.map(v => `${v.charA}+${v.charB}@${v.tile}`).join(', ')
   125→    ).toBe(0);
   126→  });
   127→
   128→  // ── TEST 2: _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles ────────────────
   129→
   130→  test('_moveCharWithCollision avoids occupied tiles but still reaches goal', async ({ page }) => {
   131→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   132→
   133→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   134→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   135→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   136→
   137→    const result = await page.evaluate((MAP_COLS) => {
   138→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   139→      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;
   140→
   141→      // Place maple as a blocker mid-path
   142→      maple.tx = 7; maple.ty = 4;
   143→
   144→      // Place nyx as the mover — wants to reach (10, 4) but maple is at (7, 4)
   145→      nyx.tx = 4; nyx.ty = 4;
   146→      nyx.prevTx = 4; nyx.prevTy = 4;
   147→      nyx.action = 'idle';
   148→      nyx.path = [];
   149→      nyx.moveProgress = 0;
   150→
   151→      const blockerKey = maple.ty * MAP_COLS + maple.tx;
   152→
   153→      // Move with collision — should avoid maple's tile
   154→      const success = engine._moveCharWithCollision(nyx, 10, 4);
   155→
   156→      if (!success) {
   157→        return { success: false, pathKeys: [], blockerKey, blockerOnPath: false };
   158→      }
   159→
   160→      const pathKeys = nyx.path.map(step => step.ty * MAP_COLS + step.tx);
   161→      const blockerOnPath = pathKeys.includes(blockerKey);
   162→
   163→      return {
   164→        success,
   165→        pathKeys,
   166→        blockerKey,
   167→        blockerOnPath,
   168→        pathLength: nyx.path.length,
   169→      };
   170→    }, MAP_COLS);
   171→
   172→    console.log('_moveCharWithCollision result:', JSON.stringify(result));
   173→
   174→    expect(result.success, 'Should find a path even with blocker in the way').toBe(true);
   175→    expect(result.blockerOnPath,
   176→      `Path must not route through blocker tile (key ${result.blockerKey})`
   177→    ).toBe(false);
   178→    expect(result.pathLength, 'Path should not be empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   179→  });
   180→
   181→  test('_moveCharWithCollision allows pathing to a tile even if it is occupied (goal exemption)', async ({ page }) => {
   182→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   183→
   184→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   185→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   186→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   187→
   188→    const result = await page.evaluate((MAP_COLS) => {
   189→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   190→      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;
   191→
   192→      // Place maple directly on the goal tile
   193→      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 4;
   194→
   195→      // Nyx wants to reach (10, 4) — the tile occupied by maple
   196→      nyx.tx = 4; nyx.ty = 4;
   197→      nyx.prevTx = 4; nyx.prevTy = 4;
   198→      nyx.action = 'idle';
   199→      nyx.path = [];
   200→      nyx.moveProgress = 0;
   201→
   202→      // _moveCharWithCollision should still allow goal tile even if occupied
   203→      const success = engine._moveCharWithCollision(nyx, 10, 4);
   204→
   205→      return {
   206→        success,
   207→        goalIsOccupied: true, // we know it is: maple.tx=10, maple.ty=4
   208→        pathLength: nyx.path.length,
   209→      };
   210→    }, MAP_COLS);
   211→
   212→    console.log('Goal-exemption result:', JSON.stringify(result));
   213→
   214→    expect(result.success,
   215→      'Should be able to path to an occupied goal tile (goal exemption must hold)'
   216→    ).toBe(true);
   217→  });
   218→
   219→  // ── TEST 3: Retry and deadlock escape ──────────────────────────────────────
   220→
   221→  test('_retryTimer counts down and _retryCount increments on repeated failures', async ({ page }) => {
   222→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   223→
   224→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   225→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   226→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   227→
   228→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
   229→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   230→      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;
   231→
   232→      // Build a fake blocked-path scenario:
   233→      // Nyx is idle with a _pendingTarget; _moveCharWithCollision will fail because
   234→      // we pack all other characters onto the only viable path tile.
   235→
   236→      // Place nyx in a corner-ish spot
   237→      nyx.tx = 1; nyx.ty = 12;
   238→      nyx.prevTx = 1; nyx.prevTy = 12;
   239→      nyx.action = 'idle';
   240→      nyx.path = [];
   241→      nyx.moveProgress = 0;
   242→
   243→      // Block the path to tile (2, 11) by placing sol and maple on adjacent tiles
   244→      sol.tx = 2; sol.ty = 12;
   245→      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 11;
   246→
   247→      const fakeObj = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');
   248→      // Set up a _pendingTarget pointing to a blocked tile
   249→      nyx._pendingTarget = { obj: fakeObj, action: 'use', tile: { tx: 3, ty: 11 } };
   250→      nyx._retryCount = 1;
   251→      nyx._retryTimer = 800; // 800ms remaining
   252→
   253→      const before = {
   254→        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,
   255→        retryTimer: nyx._retryTimer,
   256→        hasPendingTarget: !!nyx._pendingTarget,
   257→      };
   258→
   259→      // Advance 400ms — timer should partially count down but not fire yet
   260→      engine._updateCharacters(400);
   261→
   262→      const midpoint = {
   263→        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,
   264→        retryTimer: nyx._retryTimer,
   265→        hasPendingTarget: !!nyx._pendingTarget,
   266→      };
   267→
   268→      return { before, midpoint };
   269→    });
   270→
   271→    console.log('Retry timer test:', JSON.stringify(result));
   272→
   273→    expect(result.before.retryTimer, 'Timer should start at 800ms').toBe(800);
   274→    expect(result.midpoint.retryTimer,
   275→      'After 400ms, timer should have decreased by 400ms'
   276→    ).toBeLessThan(800);
   277→    expect(result.midpoint.retryTimer,
   278→      'Timer should still be > 0 (400ms of 800ms elapsed)'
   279→    ).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   280→    expect(result.midpoint.hasPendingTarget,
   281→      '_pendingTarget should still be set (timer has not fired yet)'
   282→    ).toBe(true);
   283→  });
   284→
   285→  test('_deadlockEscape fires after 3 failed retries and moves char to adjacent free tile', async ({ page }) => {
   286→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   287→
   288→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   289→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   290→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   291→
   292→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
   293→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   294→      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;
   295→
   296→      // Place nyx in the café sector with an open adjacency for escape
   297→      nyx.tx = 8; nyx.ty = 14;
   298→      nyx.prevTx = 8; nyx.prevTy = 14;
   299→      nyx.action = 'idle';
   300→      nyx.path = [];
   301→      nyx.moveProgress = 0;
   302→
   303→      // Block all neighbours EXCEPT one, to force a specific escape direction.
   304→      // Adjacent tiles to (8,14): (8,13),(8,15),(7,14),(9,14)
   305→      // Leave (9,14) free, block the rest with sol and maple
   306→      sol.tx = 8; sol.ty = 13;
   307→      maple.tx = 7; maple.ty = 14;
   308→      // (8,15) is the floor below — check if it's walkable (it may be a wall)
   309→      // For this test it doesn't matter — escape picks any free walkable tile.
   310→
   311→      const fakeObj = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');
   312→      // Set retryCount = 3 so next expiry triggers _deadlockEscape
   313→      nyx._pendingTarget = { obj: fakeObj, action: 'use', tile: { tx: 5, ty: 11 } };
   314→      nyx._retryCount = 3;
   315→      nyx._retryTimer = 10; // almost expired
   316→
   317→      const before = { tx: nyx.tx, ty: nyx.ty, retryCount: nyx._retryCount };
   318→
   319→      // Advance enough to fire the timer (10ms) and trigger escape
   320→      engine._updateCharacters(50);
   321→
   322→      const after = {
   323→        tx: Math.round(nyx.tx),
   324→        ty: Math.round(nyx.ty),
   325→        action: nyx.action,
   326→        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,
   327→      };
   328→
   329→      const moved = after.tx !== before.tx || after.ty !== before.ty;
   330→
   331→      return { before, after, moved };
   332→    });
   333→
   334→    console.log('Deadlock escape test:', JSON.stringify(result));
   335→
   336→    // After deadlock escape, character should have moved OR _pendingTarget was cleared (surrounded)
   337→    // Either outcome is valid: escape moved char, or gave up cleanly.
   338→    const escapedOrCleared = result.moved || result.after.retryCount === 0;
   339→    expect(escapedOrCleared,
   340→      'After 3 retries: character should escape to adjacent tile OR _retryCount reset to 0'
   341→    ).toBe(true);
   342→
   343→    if (result.moved) {
   344→      const dist = Math.abs(result.after.tx - result.before.tx) + Math.abs(result.after.ty - result.before.ty);
   345→      expect(dist, 'Escape should move exactly 1 tile (Manhattan distance)').toBeLessThanOrEqual(1);
   346→    }
   347→  });
   348→
   349→  // ── TEST 4: _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles ────────────────
   350→
   351→  test('_findFreeInteractTile returns an unoccupied tile when alternatives exist', async ({ page }) => {
   352→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   353→
   354→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   355→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   356→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   357→
   358→    // Run multiple trials — the function is random, so we sample several times
   359→    const TRIALS = 20;
   360→    const result = await page.evaluate((TRIALS) => {
   361→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   362→      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;
   363→
   364→      // Use cafe_espresso at (tx=2, ty=11) — single tile
   365→      const espresso = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');
   366→      if (!espresso) return { error: 'cafe_espresso not found' };
   367→
   368→      // Occupy ONE interact tile around espresso with maple.
   369→      // Adjacent walkable tiles around (2,11): typically (1,11),(3,11),(2,10),(2,12)
   370→      // We'll park maple at (1,11) — one of the interact tiles.
   371→      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 11;
   372→
   373→      // Nyx asks for a free interact tile — maple's tile should not be returned
   374→      // (because at least one other candidate tile exists)
   375→      const mapleKey = maple.ty * 16 + maple.tx; // MAP_COLS=16
   376→      const trials = [];
   377→
   378→      for (let i = 0; i < TRIALS; i++) {
   379→        const tile = engine._findFreeInteractTile(espresso.position, nyx);
   380→        if (!tile) {
   381→          trials.push({ error: 'null' });
   382→          continue;
   383→        }
   384→        const key = tile.ty * 16 + tile.tx;
   385→        trials.push({ tx: tile.tx, ty: tile.ty, isMaple: key === mapleKey });
   386→      }
   387→
   388→      return { trials, maplePos: { tx: maple.tx, ty: maple.ty }, objPos: espresso.position };
   389→    }, TRIALS);
   390→
   391→    if (result.error) {
   392→      console.log('[SKIP]', result.error);
   393→      return;
   394→    }
   395→
   396→    console.log('_findFreeInteractTile trials:', JSON.stringify(result.trials));
   397→
   398→    const nullCount = result.trials.filter(t => t.error === 'null').length;
   399→    const mapleCount = result.trials.filter(t => t.isMaple).length;
   400→
   401→    expect(nullCount,
   402→      `_findFreeInteractTile should not return null when adjacents exist (got ${nullCount}/${TRIALS} nulls)`
   403→    ).toBe(0);
   404→
   405→    expect(mapleCount,
   406→      `_findFreeInteractTile should never return maple's occupied tile when free alternatives exist ` +
   407→      `(returned maple's tile ${mapleCount}/${TRIALS} times)`
   408→    ).toBe(0);
   409→  });
   410→
   411→  // ── TEST 5: Live sim stability over 120s ──────────────────────────────────
   412→
   413→  test('no character gets permanently stuck and sim stays stable over 120s', async ({ page }) => {
   414→    test.setTimeout(60_000);
   415→
   416→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   417→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   418→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   419→
   420→    // Use manualTick() — it calls both _wanderTick() (decision-making / goal assignment)
   421→    // and _updateCharacters(tickIntervalMs) — so characters actually receive goals and move.
   422→    // 24 ticks × 5000ms/tick = 120 sim-seconds.
   423→    // "Stuck" = character never left their initial spawn tile and never entered a non-idle
   424→    // action during the entire 120s window.
   425→    const stability = await page.evaluate(() => {
   426→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   427→
   428→      // Ensure deterministic random-wander mode (not LLM-AI mode)
   429→      engine.aiMode = false;
   430→
   431→      const TICKS = 24; // 24 × 5000ms = 120 sim-seconds
   432→
   433→      // Record initial spawn positions
   434→      const initialPos = {};
   435→      for (const c of engine.characters) {
   436→        initialPos[c.id] = { tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty) };
   437→      }
   438→
   439→      const everActive = {};   // charId → true once char walked or interacted
   440→      for (const c of engine.characters) everActive[c.id] = false;
   441→
   442→      const errors = [];
   443→
   444→      for (let tick = 0; tick < TICKS; tick++) {
   445→        try {
   446→          engine.manualTick(1);
   447→        } catch (e) {
   448→          errors.push({ tick, simMs: tick * 5000, msg: String(e) });
   449→          break;
   450→        }
   451→
   452→        for (const c of engine.characters) {
   453→          if (!everActive[c.id]) {
   454→            const tx = Math.round(c.tx);
   455→            const ty = Math.round(c.ty);
   456→            // Active = moved from spawn OR in a non-idle state
   457→            if (c.action !== 'idle' ||
   458→                tx !== initialPos[c.id].tx ||
   459→                ty !== initialPos[c.id].ty) {
   460→              everActive[c.id] = true;
   461→            }
   462→          }
   463→        }
   464→      }
   465→
   466→      const stuck = Object.entries(everActive)
   467→        .filter(([, active]) => !active)
   468→        .map(([id]) => ({ id, spawn: initialPos[id] }));
   469→
   470→      return { stuck, errors, simMs: TICKS * 5000 };
   471→    });
   472→
   473→    console.log(`Sim ran for ${stability.simMs}ms sim-time`);
   474→    if (stability.errors.length > 0) console.error('Sim errors:', stability.errors);
   475→    if (stability.stuck.length > 0) console.warn('Stuck chars:', stability.stuck);
   476→
   477→    expect(stability.errors.length,
   478→      `Sim threw ${stability.errors.length} error(s): ` +
   479→      stability.errors.map(e => e.msg).join('; ')
   480→    ).toBe(0);
   481→
   482→    expect(stability.stuck.length,
   483→      `${stability.stuck.length} character(s) never left their spawn tile in 120 sim-seconds: ` +
   484→      stability.stuck.map(s => `${s.id}@(${s.spawn.tx},${s.spawn.ty})`).join(', ')
   485→    ).toBe(0);
   486→  });
   487→});
   488→

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01Th8aKFG3Qxts1Jvi7sNbmU",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-245\n     4\u2192 * Character-to-character collision avoidance.\n     5\u2192 *\n     6\u2192 * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5175) which serves\n     7\u2192 * the feature/frg-245-character-collision branch.\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 * The page does NOT expose window._gameEngine by default, so we intercept\n    10\u2192 * index.html via page.route() and inject the exposure before the script executes.\n    11\u2192 *\n    12\u2192 * Test layout:\n    13\u2192 *   1. No tile sharing \u2014 no two characters occupy the same tile when idle/interacting\n    14\u2192 *   2. _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles and preserves goal exemption\n    15\u2192 *   3. Retry and deadlock escape \u2014 _retryTimer counts down, _deadlockEscape fires at 3 failures\n    16\u2192 *   4. _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles when alternatives exist\n    17\u2192 *   5. Live sim stability \u2014 no permanently stuck characters over 120s of sim time\n    18\u2192 */\n    19\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192const LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5175';\n    22\u2192const MAP_COLS = 16; // from tilemap.js \u2014 width of tile grid\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192// \u2500\u2500 helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192/**\n    27\u2192 * Intercept index.html and inject window._gameEngine = engine so tests can\n    28\u2192 * call page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.*).\n    29\u2192 */\n    30\u2192async function injectEngineExposure(page) {\n    31\u2192  await page.route('**/', async (route) => {\n    32\u2192    const response = await route.fetch();\n    33\u2192    let body = await response.text();\n    34\u2192    body = body.replace(\n    35\u2192      '// \u2500\u2500 Initial state \u2500\u2500',\n    36\u2192      '// \u2500\u2500 Initial state (QA injection) \u2500\u2500\\nwindow._gameEngine = engine;'\n    37\u2192    );\n    38\u2192    await route.fulfill({ response, body });\n    39\u2192  });\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192/**\n    43\u2192 * Click start overlay (if visible) and optionally boost timeScale.\n    44\u2192 */\n    45\u2192async function startEngine(page, timeScale = 1) {\n    46\u2192  const overlay = page.locator('#start-overlay');\n    47\u2192  if (await overlay.isVisible()) {\n    48\u2192    await overlay.click();\n    49\u2192  }\n    50\u2192  if (timeScale !== 1) {\n    51\u2192    await page.evaluate((scale) => {\n    52\u2192      if (window._gameEngine) window._gameEngine.timeScale = scale;\n    53\u2192    }, timeScale);\n    54\u2192  }\n    55\u2192}\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192/**\n    58\u2192 * Poll engine snapshot until predicate returns truthy or timeout.\n    59\u2192 */\n    60\u2192async function pollSnapshot(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 45_000, intervalMs = 300) {\n    61\u2192  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n    62\u2192  while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n    63\u2192    const snap = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.snapshot());\n    64\u2192    if (predicate(snap)) return snap;\n    65\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n    66\u2192  }\n    67\u2192  throw new Error(`pollSnapshot timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n    68\u2192}\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192test.describe('FRG-245: Character Collision Avoidance', () => {\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 1: No tile sharing during idle/interacting \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  test('no two characters share a tile when idle or interacting over 60s', async ({ page }) => {\n    77\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    80\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    81\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n    82\u2192    await startEngine(page, 20); // 20x speed so 60 sim-seconds = ~3 real seconds\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    const violations = [];\n    85\u2192    const POLL_COUNT = 30;\n    86\u2192    const POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 300; // ~9 real seconds at 20x = 180 sim-seconds\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192    for (let i = 0; i < POLL_COUNT; i++) {\n    89\u2192      await page.waitForTimeout(POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192      const charPositions = await page.evaluate(() =>\n    92\u2192        window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n    93\u2192          id: c.id,\n    94\u2192          tx: Math.round(c.tx),\n    95\u2192          ty: Math.round(c.ty),\n    96\u2192          action: c.action,\n    97\u2192        }))\n    98\u2192      );\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192      // Only check tile sharing when characters are in a stable state (idle/interacting)\n   101\u2192      const stable = charPositions.filter(c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'interacting');\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192      for (let a = 0; a < stable.length; a++) {\n   104\u2192        for (let b = a + 1; b < stable.length; b++) {\n   105\u2192          if (stable[a].tx === stable[b].tx && stable[a].ty === stable[b].ty) {\n   106\u2192            violations.push({\n   107\u2192              poll: i,\n   108\u2192              charA: stable[a].id,\n   109\u2192              charB: stable[b].id,\n   110\u2192              tile: `(${stable[a].tx},${stable[a].ty})`,\n   111\u2192              actions: `${stable[a].action}/${stable[b].action}`,\n   112\u2192            });\n   113\u2192          }\n   114\u2192        }\n   115\u2192      }\n   116\u2192    }\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    if (violations.length > 0) {\n   119\u2192      console.error('Tile-sharing violations:', JSON.stringify(violations, null, 2));\n   120\u2192    }\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    expect(violations.length,\n   123\u2192      `Characters shared tiles on ${violations.length} poll(s): ` +\n   124\u2192      violations.map(v => `${v.charA}+${v.charB}@${v.tile}`).join(', ')\n   125\u2192    ).toBe(0);\n   126\u2192  });\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 2: _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192  test('_moveCharWithCollision avoids occupied tiles but still reaches goal', async ({ page }) => {\n   131\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   134\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   135\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate((MAP_COLS) => {\n   138\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   139\u2192      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192      // Place maple as a blocker mid-path\n   142\u2192      maple.tx = 7; maple.ty = 4;\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192      // Place nyx as the mover \u2014 wants to reach (10, 4) but maple is at (7, 4)\n   145\u2192      nyx.tx = 4; nyx.ty = 4;\n   146\u2192      nyx.prevTx = 4; nyx.prevTy = 4;\n   147\u2192      nyx.action = 'idle';\n   148\u2192      nyx.path = [];\n   149\u2192      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192      const blockerKey = maple.ty * MAP_COLS + maple.tx;\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192      // Move with collision \u2014 should avoid maple's tile\n   154\u2192      const success = engine._moveCharWithCollision(nyx, 10, 4);\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192      if (!success) {\n   157\u2192        return { success: false, pathKeys: [], blockerKey, blockerOnPath: false };\n   158\u2192      }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192      const pathKeys = nyx.path.map(step => step.ty * MAP_COLS + step.tx);\n   161\u2192      const blockerOnPath = pathKeys.includes(blockerKey);\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192      return {\n   164\u2192        success,\n   165\u2192        pathKeys,\n   166\u2192        blockerKey,\n   167\u2192        blockerOnPath,\n   168\u2192        pathLength: nyx.path.length,\n   169\u2192      };\n   170\u2192    }, MAP_COLS);\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    console.log('_moveCharWithCollision result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192    expect(result.success, 'Should find a path even with blocker in the way').toBe(true);\n   175\u2192    expect(result.blockerOnPath,\n   176\u2192      `Path must not route through blocker tile (key ${result.blockerKey})`\n   177\u2192    ).toBe(false);\n   178\u2192    expect(result.pathLength, 'Path should not be empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   179\u2192  });\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192  test('_moveCharWithCollision allows pathing to a tile even if it is occupied (goal exemption)', async ({ page }) => {\n   182\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   185\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   186\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate((MAP_COLS) => {\n   189\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   190\u2192      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192      // Place maple directly on the goal tile\n   193\u2192      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 4;\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192      // Nyx wants to reach (10, 4) \u2014 the tile occupied by maple\n   196\u2192      nyx.tx = 4; nyx.ty = 4;\n   197\u2192      nyx.prevTx = 4; nyx.prevTy = 4;\n   198\u2192      nyx.action = 'idle';\n   199\u2192      nyx.path = [];\n   200\u2192      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192      // _moveCharWithCollision should still allow goal tile even if occupied\n   203\u2192      const success = engine._moveCharWithCollision(nyx, 10, 4);\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192      return {\n   206\u2192        success,\n   207\u2192        goalIsOccupied: true, // we know it is: maple.tx=10, maple.ty=4\n   208\u2192        pathLength: nyx.path.length,\n   209\u2192      };\n   210\u2192    }, MAP_COLS);\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192    console.log('Goal-exemption result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192    expect(result.success,\n   215\u2192      'Should be able to path to an occupied goal tile (goal exemption must hold)'\n   216\u2192    ).toBe(true);\n   217\u2192  });\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 3: Retry and deadlock escape \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192  test('_retryTimer counts down and _retryCount increments on repeated failures', async ({ page }) => {\n   222\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   225\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   226\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   229\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   230\u2192      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192      // Build a fake blocked-path scenario:\n   233\u2192      // Nyx is idle with a _pendingTarget; _moveCharWithCollision will fail because\n   234\u2192      // we pack all other characters onto the only viable path tile.\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192      // Place nyx in a corner-ish spot\n   237\u2192      nyx.tx = 1; nyx.ty = 12;\n   238\u2192      nyx.prevTx = 1; nyx.prevTy = 12;\n   239\u2192      nyx.action = 'idle';\n   240\u2192      nyx.path = [];\n   241\u2192      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192      // Block the path to tile (2, 11) by placing sol and maple on adjacent tiles\n   244\u2192      sol.tx = 2; sol.ty = 12;\n   245\u2192      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 11;\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192      const fakeObj = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n   248\u2192      // Set up a _pendingTarget pointing to a blocked tile\n   249\u2192      nyx._pendingTarget = { obj: fakeObj, action: 'use', tile: { tx: 3, ty: 11 } };\n   250\u2192      nyx._retryCount = 1;\n   251\u2192      nyx._retryTimer = 800; // 800ms remaining\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192      const before = {\n   254\u2192        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n   255\u2192        retryTimer: nyx._retryTimer,\n   256\u2192        hasPendingTarget: !!nyx._pendingTarget,\n   257\u2192      };\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192      // Advance 400ms \u2014 timer should partially count down but not fire yet\n   260\u2192      engine._updateCharacters(400);\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192      const midpoint = {\n   263\u2192        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n   264\u2192        retryTimer: nyx._retryTimer,\n   265\u2192        hasPendingTarget: !!nyx._pendingTarget,\n   266\u2192      };\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192      return { before, midpoint };\n   269\u2192    });\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192    console.log('Retry timer test:', JSON.stringify(result));\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192    expect(result.before.retryTimer, 'Timer should start at 800ms').toBe(800);\n   274\u2192    expect(result.midpoint.retryTimer,\n   275\u2192      'After 400ms, timer should have decreased by 400ms'\n   276\u2192    ).toBeLessThan(800);\n   277\u2192    expect(result.midpoint.retryTimer,\n   278\u2192      'Timer should still be > 0 (400ms of 800ms elapsed)'\n   279\u2192    ).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   280\u2192    expect(result.midpoint.hasPendingTarget,\n   281\u2192      '_pendingTarget should still be set (timer has not fired yet)'\n   282\u2192    ).toBe(true);\n   283\u2192  });\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192  test('_deadlockEscape fires after 3 failed retries and moves char to adjacent free tile', async ({ page }) => {\n   286\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   289\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   290\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   293\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   294\u2192      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192      // Place nyx in the caf\u00e9 sector with an open adjacency for escape\n   297\u2192      nyx.tx = 8; nyx.ty = 14;\n   298\u2192      nyx.prevTx = 8; nyx.prevTy = 14;\n   299\u2192      nyx.action = 'idle';\n   300\u2192      nyx.path = [];\n   301\u2192      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192      // Block all neighbours EXCEPT one, to force a specific escape direction.\n   304\u2192      // Adjacent tiles to (8,14): (8,13),(8,15),(7,14),(9,14)\n   305\u2192      // Leave (9,14) free, block the rest with sol and maple\n   306\u2192      sol.tx = 8; sol.ty = 13;\n   307\u2192      maple.tx = 7; maple.ty = 14;\n   308\u2192      // (8,15) is the floor below \u2014 check if it's walkable (it may be a wall)\n   309\u2192      // For this test it doesn't matter \u2014 escape picks any free walkable tile.\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192      const fakeObj = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n   312\u2192      // Set retryCount = 3 so next expiry triggers _deadlockEscape\n   313\u2192      nyx._pendingTarget = { obj: fakeObj, action: 'use', tile: { tx: 5, ty: 11 } };\n   314\u2192      nyx._retryCount = 3;\n   315\u2192      nyx._retryTimer = 10; // almost expired\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192      const before = { tx: nyx.tx, ty: nyx.ty, retryCount: nyx._retryCount };\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192      // Advance enough to fire the timer (10ms) and trigger escape\n   320\u2192      engine._updateCharacters(50);\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192      const after = {\n   323\u2192        tx: Math.round(nyx.tx),\n   324\u2192        ty: Math.round(nyx.ty),\n   325\u2192        action: nyx.action,\n   326\u2192        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n   327\u2192      };\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192      const moved = after.tx !== before.tx || after.ty !== before.ty;\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192      return { before, after, moved };\n   332\u2192    });\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192    console.log('Deadlock escape test:', JSON.stringify(result));\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192    // After deadlock escape, character should have moved OR _pendingTarget was cleared (surrounded)\n   337\u2192    // Either outcome is valid: escape moved char, or gave up cleanly.\n   338\u2192    const escapedOrCleared = result.moved || result.after.retryCount === 0;\n   339\u2192    expect(escapedOrCleared,\n   340\u2192      'After 3 retries: character should escape to adjacent tile OR _retryCount reset to 0'\n   341\u2192    ).toBe(true);\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192    if (result.moved) {\n   344\u2192      const dist = Math.abs(result.after.tx - result.before.tx) + Math.abs(result.after.ty - result.before.ty);\n   345\u2192      expect(dist, 'Escape should move exactly 1 tile (Manhattan distance)').toBeLessThanOrEqual(1);\n   346\u2192    }\n   347\u2192  });\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 4: _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192  test('_findFreeInteractTile returns an unoccupied tile when alternatives exist', async ({ page }) => {\n   352\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   355\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   356\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192    // Run multiple trials \u2014 the function is random, so we sample several times\n   359\u2192    const TRIALS = 20;\n   360\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate((TRIALS) => {\n   361\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   362\u2192      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192      // Use cafe_espresso at (tx=2, ty=11) \u2014 single tile\n   365\u2192      const espresso = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n   366\u2192      if (!espresso) return { error: 'cafe_espresso not found' };\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192      // Occupy ONE interact tile around espresso with maple.\n   369\u2192      // Adjacent walkable tiles around (2,11): typically (1,11),(3,11),(2,10),(2,12)\n   370\u2192      // We'll park maple at (1,11) \u2014 one of the interact tiles.\n   371\u2192      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 11;\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192      // Nyx asks for a free interact tile \u2014 maple's tile should not be returned\n   374\u2192      // (because at least one other candidate tile exists)\n   375\u2192      const mapleKey = maple.ty * 16 + maple.tx; // MAP_COLS=16\n   376\u2192      const trials = [];\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192      for (let i = 0; i < TRIALS; i++) {\n   379\u2192        const tile = engine._findFreeInteractTile(espresso.position, nyx);\n   380\u2192        if (!tile) {\n   381\u2192          trials.push({ error: 'null' });\n   382\u2192          continue;\n   383\u2192        }\n   384\u2192        const key = tile.ty * 16 + tile.tx;\n   385\u2192        trials.push({ tx: tile.tx, ty: tile.ty, isMaple: key === mapleKey });\n   386\u2192      }\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192      return { trials, maplePos: { tx: maple.tx, ty: maple.ty }, objPos: espresso.position };\n   389\u2192    }, TRIALS);\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192    if (result.error) {\n   392\u2192      console.log('[SKIP]', result.error);\n   393\u2192      return;\n   394\u2192    }\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192    console.log('_findFreeInteractTile trials:', JSON.stringify(result.trials));\n   397\u2192\n   398\u2192    const nullCount = result.trials.filter(t => t.error === 'null').length;\n   399\u2192    const mapleCount = result.trials.filter(t => t.isMaple).length;\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192    expect(nullCount,\n   402\u2192      `_findFreeInteractTile should not return null when adjacents exist (got ${nullCount}/${TRIALS} nulls)`\n   403\u2192    ).toBe(0);\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192    expect(mapleCount,\n   406\u2192      `_findFreeInteractTile should never return maple's occupied tile when free alternatives exist ` +\n   407\u2192      `(returned maple's tile ${mapleCount}/${TRIALS} times)`\n   408\u2192    ).toBe(0);\n   409\u2192  });\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Live sim stability over 120s \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192  test('no character gets permanently stuck and sim stays stable over 120s', async ({ page }) => {\n   414\u2192    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n   415\u2192\n   416\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   417\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   418\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   419\u2192\n   420\u2192    // Use manualTick() \u2014 it calls both _wanderTick() (decision-making / goal assignment)\n   421\u2192    // and _updateCharacters(tickIntervalMs) \u2014 so characters actually receive goals and move.\n   422\u2192    // 24 ticks \u00d7 5000ms/tick = 120 sim-seconds.\n   423\u2192    // \"Stuck\" = character never left their initial spawn tile and never entered a non-idle\n   424\u2192    // action during the entire 120s window.\n   425\u2192    const stability = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   426\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192      // Ensure deterministic random-wander mode (not LLM-AI mode)\n   429\u2192      engine.aiMode = false;\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192      const TICKS = 24; // 24 \u00d7 5000ms = 120 sim-seconds\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192      // Record initial spawn positions\n   434\u2192      const initialPos = {};\n   435\u2192      for (const c of engine.characters) {\n   436\u2192        initialPos[c.id] = { tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty) };\n   437\u2192      }\n   438\u2192\n   439\u2192      const everActive = {};   // charId \u2192 true once char walked or interacted\n   440\u2192      for (const c of engine.characters) everActive[c.id] = false;\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192      const errors = [];\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192      for (let tick = 0; tick < TICKS; tick++) {\n   445\u2192        try {\n   446\u2192          engine.manualTick(1);\n   447\u2192        } catch (e) {\n   448\u2192          errors.push({ tick, simMs: tick * 5000, msg: String(e) });\n   449\u2192          break;\n   450\u2192        }\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192        for (const c of engine.characters) {\n   453\u2192          if (!everActive[c.id]) {\n   454\u2192            const tx = Math.round(c.tx);\n   455\u2192            const ty = Math.round(c.ty);\n   456\u2192            // Active = moved from spawn OR in a non-idle state\n   457\u2192            if (c.action !== 'idle' ||\n   458\u2192                tx !== initialPos[c.id].tx ||\n   459\u2192                ty !== initialPos[c.id].ty) {\n   460\u2192              everActive[c.id] = true;\n   461\u2192            }\n   462\u2192          }\n   463\u2192        }\n   464\u2192      }\n   465\u2192\n   466\u2192      const stuck = Object.entries(everActive)\n   467\u2192        .filter(([, active]) => !active)\n   468\u2192        .map(([id]) => ({ id, spawn: initialPos[id] }));\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192      return { stuck, errors, simMs: TICKS * 5000 };\n   471\u2192    });\n   472\u2192\n   473\u2192    console.log(`Sim ran for ${stability.simMs}ms sim-time`);\n   474\u2192    if (stability.errors.length > 0) console.error('Sim errors:', stability.errors);\n   475\u2192    if (stability.stuck.length > 0) console.warn('Stuck chars:', stability.stuck);\n   476\u2192\n   477\u2192    expect(stability.errors.length,\n   478\u2192      `Sim threw ${stability.errors.length} error(s): ` +\n   479\u2192      stability.errors.map(e => e.msg).join('; ')\n   480\u2192    ).toBe(0);\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192    expect(stability.stuck.length,\n   483\u2192      `${stability.stuck.length} character(s) never left their spawn tile in 120 sim-seconds: ` +\n   484\u2192      stability.stuck.map(s => `${s.id}@(${s.spawn.tx},${s.spawn.ty})`).join(', ')\n   485\u2192    ).toBe(0);\n   486\u2192  });\n   487\u2192});\n   488\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

USERMESSAGE #20
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * Shared helpers for AI Aquarium QA tests.
     4→ * All tests run against the deployed BASE_URL via window._gameEngine.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Wait for Phaser to finish booting and the SimulationEngine to be attached.
     9→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    10→ * @param {number} [timeout=20000]
    11→ */
    12→async function waitForEngine(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    13→  await page.waitForFunction(
    14→    () => window._gameEngine &&
    15→          window._gameEngine.characters &&
    16→          window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,
    17→    { timeout }
    18→  );
    19→}
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Read the live engine state.
    23→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    24→ */
    25→async function getEngineState(page) {
    26→  return page.evaluate(() => {
    27→    const eng = window._gameEngine;
    28→    return {
    29→      simTime: eng.getSimTimeString(),
    30→      aiEnabled: eng.aiEngine?.enabled ?? false,
    31→      characters: eng.characters.map(c => ({
    32→        name: c.name,
    33→        label: c.label,
    34→        tx: c.tx,
    35→        ty: c.ty,
    36→        action: c.action,
    37→        mood: c.mood,
    38→        energy: c.energy,
    39→      })),
    40→      objectStates: eng.objectEngine.getAll().map(o => ({
    41→        id: o.id,
    42→        label: o.label,
    43→        state: o.state,
    44→      })),
    45→    };
    46→  });
    47→}
    48→
    49→/**
    50→ * Poll engine state until predicate returns truthy, or timeout.
    51→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    52→ * @param {(state: object) => boolean} predicate
    53→ * @param {number} [timeoutMs=30000]
    54→ * @param {number} [intervalMs=500]
    55→ */
    56→async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {
    57→  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
    58→  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
    59→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    60→    if (predicate(state)) return state;
    61→    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);
    62→  }
    63→  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);
    64→}
    65→
    66→/**
    67→ * Wait for /debug.html dashboard to finish loading.
    68→ * Shared by m1-debug-dashboard, slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic specs.
    69→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    70→ * @param {number} [timeout=20000]
    71→ */
    72→async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    73→  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });
    74→  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });
    75→  await page.waitForFunction(
    76→    () => {
    77→      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
    78→      if (cards.length < 3) return false;
    79→      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');
    80→    },
    81→    { timeout }
    82→  );
    83→}
    84→
    85→module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard };
    86→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192/**\n    67\u2192 * Wait page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n    74\u2192  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n    75\u2192  await page.waitForFunction(\n    76\u2192    () => {\n      8→  testDir: './tests',
     9→  timeout: 60_000,
    10→  expect: { timeout: 15_000 },
    11→  fullyParallel: false,
    12→  retries: 1,
    13→  reporter: [['list'], ['json', { outputFile: 'test-results/results.json' }]],
    14→  use: {

    16→    headless: true,
    17→    viewport: { width: 1280, height: 960 },

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #42
Interesting! The source doesn't have `_moveCharWithCollision`, `_findFreeInteractTile`, `_retryTimer`, `_retryCount`, `_deadlockEscape`, `_pendingTarget`, `manualTick`, `timeScale`, or `snapshot`. This means the existing tests in the collision-avoidance.spec.js file were written for API methods that don't exist in the actual implementation! The task instructions say: - `Engine._buildDynamicWalkable(excludeChar)` — yes, this exists - `Engine._findAdjacentWalkable(tx, ty, walkable?)` — yes, this exists - `Character.moveTo(goalTx, goalTy, walkable?)` — need to check - `Character._failedMoveCount` — yes, this exists The deadlock escape mechanism kicks in after 3 failed wander attempts, stepping to any free adjacent tile. Wander retry backoff is 500-1000ms instead of the original 2s. The `_doWander`, `_handleTalkArrival`, `_updateTalkRoute`, and `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` methods all use the dynamic grid, and `AIEngine._applyDecision` builds it for move, interact, and talk actions. But the existing tests don't align with what's actually implemented. The test file is calling methods that don't exist in the source code — `_moveCharWithCollision`, `_findFreeInteractTile`, `manualTick`, and `snapshot` — and checking for properties like `_retryCount`, `_retryTimer`, and `_pendingTarget` that aren't there. I need to look at the actual Engine.js implementation to understand what the real API is and rewrite the tests accordingly.
