ASSISTANTMESSAGE #139
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/collision-avoidance.spec.js",
  "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-245\n * Character-to-character collision avoidance.\n *\n * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5175) which serves\n * the feature/frg-245-character-collision-avoidance branch.\n *\n * window._gameEngine is set unconditionally in scene.js create() so no\n * route injection is needed.\n *\n * Actual API (verified against source):\n *   engine._buildDynamicWalkable(excludeChar)      \u2014 clones static grid, marks other chars blocked\n *   engine._findAdjacentWalkable(tx, ty, walkable) \u2014 returns first free cardinal neighbor\n *   engine._doWander(char)                         \u2014 runs wander logic inc. deadlock escape\n *   engine.update(deltaMs)                         \u2014 advances one sim tick\n *   char.moveTo(goalTx, goalTy, walkable?)         \u2014 A* path on given grid; returns boolean\n *   char._failedMoveCount                          \u2014 consecutive wander failures; reset on success\n *   char.wanderTimer                               \u2014 ms until next wander attempt\n *   engine.walkable[row][col]                      \u2014 static walkable grid (boolean[][])\n *   engine.characters                              \u2014 [Maple(Amelia), Sol(Lucy), Nyx(Ash)]\n *   engine.objectEngine.getById(id)                \u2014 valid IDs: stove,fridge,tea_set,kotatsu,etc.\n *\n * Walkable grid: 19 cols \u00d7 13 rows.\n *   Interior floor: col 1\u201317, row 2\u20139 (minus object tiles).\n *   Object tiles (non-walkable): stove(3,3), fridge(2,3), tea_set(5,5), kotatsu(6,6),\n *     tv(9,3), bookshelf(15,3), desk(14,5), bed(16,5), easel(2,7), bonsai(11,8).\n *\n * Test layout:\n *   1. No tile sharing \u2014 no two chars idle/interacting on the same tile over 30+ poll samples\n *   2. _buildDynamicWalkable \u2014 other chars blocked, own tile walkable, static grid unchanged\n *   3. moveTo with dynamic grid \u2014 path routes around a blocking character's tile\n *   4. Deadlock escape \u2014 _failedMoveCount resets to 0 after third wander failure\n *   5. Live sim stability \u2014 no crashes and all chars become active within 120s sim-time\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\nconst LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5175';\n\n// \u2500\u2500 helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/**\n * Wait for the SimulationEngine to be fully initialised with 3 characters.\n */\nasync function waitForEngine(page) {\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n    { timeout: 15_000 }\n  );\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest.describe('FRG-245: Character Collision Avoidance', () => {\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 1: No tile sharing during idle/interacting \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('no two characters share a tile when idle or interacting over 30 poll samples', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Let the Phaser game loop run naturally; poll character positions.\n    // 30 polls \u00d7 300 ms = ~9 real seconds of simulation coverage.\n    const violations = [];\n    const POLL_COUNT   = 30;\n    const POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 300;\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < POLL_COUNT; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n\n      const charPositions = await page.evaluate(() =>\n        window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n          label:  c.label,\n          tx:     Math.round(c.tx),\n          ty:     Math.round(c.ty),\n          action: c.action,\n        }))\n      );\n\n      // Only check when characters are in a stable state (not mid-walk)\n      const stable = charPositions.filter(\n        c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'interacting' || c.action === 'talking'\n      );\n\n      for (let a = 0; a < stable.length; a++) {\n        for (let b = a + 1; b < stable.length; b++) {\n          if (stable[a].tx === stable[b].tx && stable[a].ty === stable[b].ty) {\n            violations.push({\n              poll:    i,\n              charA:   stable[a].label,\n              charB:   stable[b].label,\n              tile:    `(${stable[a].tx},${stable[a].ty})`,\n              actions: `${stable[a].action}/${stable[b].action}`,\n            });\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (violations.length > 0) {\n      console.error('Tile-sharing violations:', JSON.stringify(violations, null, 2));\n    }\n\n    expect(violations.length,\n      `Characters shared tiles on ${violations.length} poll(s): ` +\n      violations.map(v => `${v.charA}+${v.charB}@${v.tile}`).join(', ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 2: _buildDynamicWalkable correctness \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_buildDynamicWalkable marks other chars blocked, preserves own tile, leaves static grid unchanged', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      const solTx   = Math.round(sol.tx);\n      const solTy   = Math.round(sol.ty);\n      const nyxTx   = Math.round(nyx.tx);\n      const nyxTy   = Math.round(nyx.ty);\n      const mapleTx = Math.round(maple.tx);\n      const mapleTy = Math.round(maple.ty);\n\n      // Build dynamic grid treating all others as obstacles for maple\n      const dynWalkable = engine._buildDynamicWalkable(maple);\n\n      return {\n        // Other characters' tiles should be blocked in the dynamic grid\n        solBlocked:  dynWalkable[solTy]?.[solTx]   === false,\n        nyxBlocked:  dynWalkable[nyxTy]?.[nyxTx]   === false,\n        // Calling character's own tile must stay walkable\n        mapleWalkable: dynWalkable[mapleTy]?.[mapleTx] === true,\n        // Static grid must not have been modified\n        staticSolTile:   engine.walkable[solTy]?.[solTx]   === true,\n        staticNyxTile:   engine.walkable[nyxTy]?.[nyxTx]   === true,\n        staticMapleTile: engine.walkable[mapleTy]?.[mapleTx] === true,\n        // Sanity: dynamic grid is a separate object (deep clone)\n        isDifferentObject: dynWalkable !== engine.walkable,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('_buildDynamicWalkable result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.solBlocked,\n      'Sol\\'s tile should be blocked in the dynamic grid built for Maple'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.nyxBlocked,\n      'Nyx\\'s tile should be blocked in the dynamic grid built for Maple'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.mapleWalkable,\n      'Maple\\'s own tile must remain walkable in the dynamic grid'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.staticSolTile,\n      '_buildDynamicWalkable must not modify the static walkable grid (Sol\\'s tile)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.staticNyxTile,\n      '_buildDynamicWalkable must not modify the static walkable grid (Nyx\\'s tile)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.isDifferentObject,\n      'Dynamic walkable must be a separate array, not the same reference as engine.walkable'\n    ).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 3: moveTo routes around a blocking character's tile \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('moveTo with dynamic walkable avoids the blocking character\\'s tile', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place maple at (1, 2) \u2014 walkable left edge, row 2\n      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 2;\n      maple.prevTx = 1; maple.prevTy = 2;\n      maple.path = []; maple.moveProgress = 0; maple.action = 'idle';\n\n      // Place sol (blocker) at (6, 2) \u2014 directly on the straight-line path\n      sol.tx = 6; sol.ty = 2;\n\n      // Build dynamic walkable: sol's tile (6,2) is blocked\n      const dynWalkable = engine._buildDynamicWalkable(maple);\n\n      const blockerBlocked = dynWalkable[sol.ty]?.[sol.tx] === false;\n\n      // moveTo goal (12, 2) \u2014 A* must route around (6,2)\n      const success = maple.moveTo(12, 2, dynWalkable);\n\n      if (!success) {\n        return { success, blockerBlocked, blockerOnPath: false, pathLength: 0, path: [] };\n      }\n\n      const blockerKey = `${sol.ty}_${sol.tx}`;\n      const pathKeys   = maple.path.map(s => `${s.ty}_${s.tx}`);\n      const blockerOnPath = pathKeys.includes(blockerKey);\n\n      return {\n        success,\n        blockerBlocked,\n        blockerOnPath,\n        pathLength: maple.path.length,\n        // First few steps for debugging\n        pathStart: maple.path.slice(0, 5),\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('moveTo dynamic-walkable result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.blockerBlocked,\n      'Sol\\'s tile should be marked blocked in the dynamic walkable grid'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.success,\n      'moveTo should find an alternative path even with blocker at (6,2)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.blockerOnPath,\n      `Path must not route through blocker tile (6,2). Path: ${JSON.stringify(result.pathStart)}`\n    ).toBe(false);\n\n    expect(result.pathLength,\n      'Path must have at least one step'\n    ).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 4: Deadlock escape after 3 wander failures \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_failedMoveCount resets to 0 after third consecutive wander failure (deadlock escape)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place maple in an open area\n      maple.tx = 8; maple.ty = 4;\n      maple.prevTx = 8; maple.prevTy = 4;\n      maple.path = []; maple.moveProgress = 0; maple.action = 'idle';\n\n      // Seed counter: two previous failures already recorded\n      maple._failedMoveCount = 2;\n\n      // Override moveTo to always return false \u2014 simulates all paths blocked\n      const originalMoveTo = maple.moveTo.bind(maple);\n      let callCount = 0;\n      maple.moveTo = function() {\n        callCount++;\n        return false;\n      };\n\n      const countBefore = maple._failedMoveCount; // 2\n\n      // _doWander: 15 random-tile attempts all fail \u2192 _failedMoveCount 2\u21923 \u2192 escape fires \u2192 reset to 0\n      engine._doWander(maple);\n\n      const countAfter = maple._failedMoveCount;\n\n      // Restore so the engine is not permanently broken\n      maple.moveTo = originalMoveTo;\n\n      return { countBefore, countAfter, callCount };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Deadlock escape result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.countBefore,\n      '_failedMoveCount should be 2 before the wander attempt'\n    ).toBe(2);\n\n    expect(result.countAfter,\n      'After third wander failure, _failedMoveCount must reset to 0 (deadlock escape fired)'\n    ).toBe(0);\n\n    expect(result.callCount,\n      'moveTo should have been called at least once during the wander attempt'\n    ).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Live sim stability over 120s sim-time \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('no character stays permanently stuck and sim runs without errors over 120s sim-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Drive 120s of sim-time synchronously via engine.update(). Each call advances\n    // 5000ms of sim-time; Phaser's rAF loop is blocked during the evaluate() call\n    // so there is no double-tick. AI is disabled to avoid network requests.\n    const stability = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n\n      // Disable AI engine so no async LLM decisions fire during the driven ticks\n      engine.aiEngine = null;\n\n      const DELTA = 5000; // ms of sim-time per tick\n      const TICKS = 24;   // 24 \u00d7 5000ms = 120 sim-seconds\n\n      // Snapshot initial positions using label (characters have no .id field)\n      const initialPos = {};\n      for (const c of engine.characters) {\n        initialPos[c.label] = { tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty) };\n      }\n\n      // Track whether each character ever left its spawn tile or entered a non-idle state\n      const everActive = {};\n      for (const c of engine.characters) everActive[c.label] = false;\n\n      const errors = [];\n\n      for (let tick = 0; tick < TICKS; tick++) {\n        try {\n          engine.update(DELTA);\n        } catch (e) {\n          errors.push({ tick, simMs: tick * DELTA, msg: String(e) });\n          break;\n        }\n\n        for (const c of engine.characters) {\n          if (!everActive[c.label]) {\n            const tx = Math.round(c.tx);\n            const ty = Math.round(c.ty);\n            if (\n              c.action !== 'idle' ||\n              tx !== initialPos[c.label].tx ||\n              ty !== initialPos[c.label].ty\n            ) {\n              everActive[c.label] = true;\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      const stuck = Object.entries(everActive)\n        .filter(([, active]) => !active)\n        .map(([label]) => ({ label, spawn: initialPos[label] }));\n\n      return { stuck, errors, simMs: TICKS * DELTA };\n    });\n\n    console.log(`Sim ran for ${stability.simMs}ms sim-time`);\n    if (stability.errors.length > 0) console.error('Sim errors:', stability.errors);\n    if (stability.stuck.length > 0) console.warn('Stuck chars:', stability.stuck);\n\n    expect(stability.errors.length,\n      `Sim threw ${stability.errors.length} error(s): ` +\n      stability.errors.map(e => e.msg).join('; ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n\n    expect(stability.stuck.length,\n      `${stability.stuck.length} character(s) never left their spawn tile in 120 sim-seconds: ` +\n      stability.stuck.map(s => `${s.label}@(${s.spawn.tx},${s.spawn.ty})`).join(', ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n});\n"
}

{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01PPJUAmwvaEsqXTM7UdboE7",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/collision-avoidance.spec.js",
      "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-245\n * Character-to-character collision avoidance.\n *\n * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5175) which serves\n * the feature/frg-245-character-collision-avoidance branch.\n *\n * window._gameEngine is set unconditionally in scene.js create() so no\n * route injection is needed.\n *\n * Actual API (verified against source):\n *   engine._buildDynamicWalkable(excludeChar)      \u2014 clones static grid, marks other chars blocked\n *   engine._findAdjacentWalkable(tx, ty, walkable) \u2014 returns first free cardinal neighbor\n *   engine._doWander(char)                         \u2014 runs wander logic inc. deadlock escape\n *   engine.update(deltaMs)                         \u2014 advances one sim tick\n *   char.moveTo(goalTx, goalTy, walkable?)         \u2014 A* path on given grid; returns boolean\n *   char._failedMoveCount                          \u2014 consecutive wander failures; reset on success\n *   char.wanderTimer                               \u2014 ms until next wander attempt\n *   engine.walkable[row][col]                      \u2014 static walkable grid (boolean[][])\n *   engine.characters                              \u2014 [Maple(Amelia), Sol(Lucy), Nyx(Ash)]\n *   engine.objectEngine.getById(id)                \u2014 valid IDs: stove,fridge,tea_set,kotatsu,etc.\n *\n * Walkable grid: 19 cols \u00d7 13 rows.\n *   Interior floor: col 1\u201317, row 2\u20139 (minus object tiles).\n *   Object tiles (non-walkable): stove(3,3), fridge(2,3), tea_set(5,5), kotatsu(6,6),\n *     tv(9,3), bookshelf(15,3), desk(14,5), bed(16,5), easel(2,7), bonsai(11,8).\n *\n * Test layout:\n *   1. No tile sharing \u2014 no two chars idle/interacting on the same tile over 30+ poll samples\n *   2. _buildDynamicWalkable \u2014 other chars blocked, own tile walkable, static grid unchanged\n *   3. moveTo with dynamic grid \u2014 path routes around a blocking character's tile\n *   4. Deadlock escape \u2014 _failedMoveCount resets to 0 after third wander failure\n *   5. Live sim stability \u2014 no crashes and all chars become active within 120s sim-time\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\nconst LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5175';\n\n// \u2500\u2500 helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/**\n * Wait for the SimulationEngine to be fully initialised with 3 characters.\n */\nasync function waitForEngine(page) {\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n    { timeout: 15_000 }\n  );\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest.describe('FRG-245: Character Collision Avoidance', () => {\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 1: No tile sharing during idle/interacting \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('no two characters share a tile when idle or interacting over 30 poll samples', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Let the Phaser game loop run naturally; poll character positions.\n    // 30 polls \u00d7 300 ms = ~9 real seconds of simulation coverage.\n    const violations = [];\n    const POLL_COUNT   = 30;\n    const POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 300;\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < POLL_COUNT; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n\n      const charPositions = await page.evaluate(() =>\n        window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n          label:  c.label,\n          tx:     Math.round(c.tx),\n          ty:     Math.round(c.ty),\n          action: c.action,\n        }))\n      );\n\n      // Only check when characters are in a stable state (not mid-walk)\n      const stable = charPositions.filter(\n        c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'interacting' || c.action === 'talking'\n      );\n\n      for (let a = 0; a < stable.length; a++) {\n        for (let b = a + 1; b < stable.length; b++) {\n          if (stable[a].tx === stable[b].tx && stable[a].ty === stable[b].ty) {\n            violations.push({\n              poll:    i,\n              charA:   stable[a].label,\n              charB:   stable[b].label,\n              tile:    `(${stable[a].tx},${stable[a].ty})`,\n              actions: `${stable[a].action}/${stable[b].action}`,\n            });\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (violations.length > 0) {\n      console.error('Tile-sharing violations:', JSON.stringify(violations, null, 2));\n    }\n\n    expect(violations.length,\n      `Characters shared tiles on ${violations.length} poll(s): ` +\n      violations.map(v => `${v.charA}+${v.charB}@${v.tile}`).join(', ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 2: _buildDynamicWalkable correctness \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_buildDynamicWalkable marks other chars blocked, preserves own tile, leaves static grid unchanged', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      const solTx   = Math.round(sol.tx);\n      const solTy   = Math.round(sol.ty);\n      const nyxTx   = Math.round(nyx.tx);\n      const nyxTy   = Math.round(nyx.ty);\n      const mapleTx = Math.round(maple.tx);\n      const mapleTy = Math.round(maple.ty);\n\n      // Build dynamic grid treating all others as obstacles for maple\n      const dynWalkable = engine._buildDynamicWalkable(maple);\n\n      return {\n        // Other characters' tiles should be blocked in the dynamic grid\n        solBlocked:  dynWalkable[solTy]?.[solTx]   === false,\n        nyxBlocked:  dynWalkable[nyxTy]?.[nyxTx]   === false,\n        // Calling character's own tile must stay walkable\n        mapleWalkable: dynWalkable[mapleTy]?.[mapleTx] === true,\n        // Static grid must not have been modified\n        staticSolTile:   engine.walkable[solTy]?.[solTx]   === true,\n        staticNyxTile:   engine.walkable[nyxTy]?.[nyxTx]   === true,\n        staticMapleTile: engine.walkable[mapleTy]?.[mapleTx] === true,\n        // Sanity: dynamic grid is a separate object (deep clone)\n        isDifferentObject: dynWalkable !== engine.walkable,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('_buildDynamicWalkable result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.solBlocked,\n      'Sol\\'s tile should be blocked in the dynamic grid built for Maple'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.nyxBlocked,\n      'Nyx\\'s tile should be blocked in the dynamic grid built for Maple'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.mapleWalkable,\n      'Maple\\'s own tile must remain walkable in the dynamic grid'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.staticSolTile,\n      '_buildDynamicWalkable must not modify the static walkable grid (Sol\\'s tile)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.staticNyxTile,\n      '_buildDynamicWalkable must not modify the static walkable grid (Nyx\\'s tile)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.isDifferentObject,\n      'Dynamic walkable must be a separate array, not the same reference as engine.walkable'\n    ).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 3: moveTo routes around a blocking character's tile \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('moveTo with dynamic walkable avoids the blocking character\\'s tile', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place maple at (1, 2) \u2014 walkable left edge, row 2\n      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 2;\n      maple.prevTx = 1; maple.prevTy = 2;\n      maple.path = []; maple.moveProgress = 0; maple.action = 'idle';\n\n      // Place sol (blocker) at (6, 2) \u2014 directly on the straight-line path\n      sol.tx = 6; sol.ty = 2;\n\n      // Build dynamic walkable: sol's tile (6,2) is blocked\n      const dynWalkable = engine._buildDynamicWalkable(maple);\n\n      const blockerBlocked = dynWalkable[sol.ty]?.[sol.tx] === false;\n\n      // moveTo goal (12, 2) \u2014 A* must route around (6,2)\n      const success = maple.moveTo(12, 2, dynWalkable);\n\n      if (!success) {\n        return { success, blockerBlocked, blockerOnPath: false, pathLength: 0, path: [] };\n      }\n\n      const blockerKey = `${sol.ty}_${sol.tx}`;\n      const pathKeys   = maple.path.map(s => `${s.ty}_${s.tx}`);\n      const blockerOnPath = pathKeys.includes(blockerKey);\n\n      return {\n        success,\n        blockerBlocked,\n        blockerOnPath,\n        pathLength: maple.path.length,\n        // First few steps for debugging\n        pathStart: maple.path.slice(0, 5),\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('moveTo dynamic-walkable result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.blockerBlocked,\n      'Sol\\'s tile should be marked blocked in the dynamic walkable grid'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.success,\n      'moveTo should find an alternative path even with blocker at (6,2)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    expect(result.blockerOnPath,\n      `Path must not route through blocker tile (6,2). Path: ${JSON.stringify(result.pathStart)}`\n    ).toBe(false);\n\n    expect(result.pathLength,\n      'Path must have at least one step'\n    ).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 4: Deadlock escape after 3 wander failures \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_failedMoveCount resets to 0 after third consecutive wander failure (deadlock escape)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place maple in an open area\n      maple.tx = 8; maple.ty = 4;\n      maple.prevTx = 8; maple.prevTy = 4;\n      maple.path = []; maple.moveProgress = 0; maple.action = 'idle';\n\n      // Seed counter: two previous failures already recorded\n      maple._failedMoveCount = 2;\n\n      // Override moveTo to always return false \u2014 simulates all paths blocked\n      const originalMoveTo = maple.moveTo.bind(maple);\n      let callCount = 0;\n      maple.moveTo = function() {\n        callCount++;\n        return false;\n      };\n\n      const countBefore = maple._failedMoveCount; // 2\n\n      // _doWander: 15 random-tile attempts all fail \u2192 _failedMoveCount 2\u21923 \u2192 escape fires \u2192 reset to 0\n      engine._doWander(maple);\n\n      const countAfter = maple._failedMoveCount;\n\n      // Restore so the engine is not permanently broken\n      maple.moveTo = originalMoveTo;\n\n      return { countBefore, countAfter, callCount };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Deadlock escape result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.countBefore,\n      '_failedMoveCount should be 2 before the wander attempt'\n    ).toBe(2);\n\n    expect(result.countAfter,\n      'After third wander failure, _failedMoveCount must reset to 0 (deadlock escape fired)'\n    ).toBe(0);\n\n    expect(result.callCount,\n      'moveTo should have been called at least once during the wander attempt'\n    ).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Live sim stability over 120s sim-time \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('no character stays permanently stuck and sim runs without errors over 120s sim-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Drive 120s of sim-time synchronously via engine.update(). Each call advances\n    // 5000ms of sim-time; Phaser's rAF loop is blocked during the evaluate() call\n    // so there is no double-tick. AI is disabled to avoid network requests.\n    const stability = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n\n      // Disable AI engine so no async LLM decisions fire during the driven ticks\n      engine.aiEngine = null;\n\n      const DELTA = 5000; // ms of sim-time per tick\n      const TICKS = 24;   // 24 \u00d7 5000ms = 120 sim-seconds\n\n      // Snapshot initial positions using label (characters have no .id field)\n      const initialPos = {};\n      for (const c of engine.characters) {\n        initialPos[c.label] = { tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty) };\n      }\n\n      // Track whether each character ever left its spawn tile or entered a non-idle state\n      const everActive = {};\n      for (const c of engine.characters) everActive[c.label] = false;\n\n      const errors = [];\n\n      for (let tick = 0; tick < TICKS; tick++) {\n        try {\n          engine.update(DELTA);\n        } catch (e) {\n          errors.push({ tick, simMs: tick * DELTA, msg: String(e) });\n          break;\n        }\n\n        for (const c of engine.characters) {\n          if (!everActive[c.label]) {\n            const tx = Math.round(c.tx);\n            const ty = Math.round(c.ty);\n            if (\n              c.action !== 'idle' ||\n              tx !== initialPos[c.label].tx ||\n              ty !== initialPos[c.label].ty\n            ) {\n              everActive[c.label] = true;\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      const stuck = Object.entries(everActive)\n        .filter(([, active]) => !active)\n        .map(([label]) => ({ label, spawn: initialPos[label] }));\n\n      return { stuck, errors, simMs: TICKS * DELTA };\n    });\n\n    console.log(`Sim ran for ${stability.simMs}ms sim-time`);\n    if (stability.errors.length > 0) console.error('Sim errors:', stability.errors);\n    if (stability.stuck.length > 0) console.warn('Stuck chars:', stability.stuck);\n\n    expect(stability.errors.length,\n      `Sim threw ${stability.errors.length} error(s): ` +\n      stability.errors.map(e => e.msg).join('; ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n\n    expect(stability.stuck.length,\n      `${stability.stuck.length} character(s) never left their spawn tile in 120 sim-seconds: ` +\n      stability.stuck.map(s => `${s.label}@(${s.spawn.tx},${s.spawn.ty})`).join(', ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

{
  "command": "git add tests/regression/collision-avoidance.spec.js && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(qa): FRG-245 collision avoidance regression suite \u2014 rewrite to match actual API\n\nThe initial spec was written against a hypothetical API (_moveCharWithCollision,\n_findFreeInteractTile, _retryTimer/_retryCount/_pendingTarget, manualTick, aiMode,\nsnapshot, objectEngine.get) that does not exist in the implementation. This commit\naligns all 5 tests with the real Engine/Character API confirmed by reading source:\n\n- _buildDynamicWalkable / _findAdjacentWalkable / _doWander on Engine\n- char.moveTo(tx, ty, walkable?) and char._failedMoveCount on Character\n- engine.update(deltaMs) drives the sim; aiEngine=null disables AI for stability test\n- objectEngine.getById() (not get()); valid object IDs from OBJECT_DEFS\n- char.label used as identifier (characters have no .id field)\n- MAP_COLS corrected to 19 (was 16); no start-overlay or timeScale on this branch\n\nAll 5 tests pass: tile-sharing, _buildDynamicWalkable correctness, moveTo detour,\ndeadlock escape (_failedMoveCount reset), and 120s live sim stability.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}

This commit\naligns all 5 tests with the real Engine/Character API confirmed by reading source:\n\n- _buildDynamicWalkable / _findAdjacentWalkable / _doWander on Engine\n- char.moveTo(tx, ty, walkable?) and char._failedMoveCount on Character\n- engine.update(deltaMs) drives the sim; aiEngine=null disables AI for stability test\n- objectEngine.getById() (not get()); valid object IDs from OBJECT_DEFS\n- char.label used as identifier (characters have no .id field)\n- MAP_COLS corrected to 19 (was 16); no start-overlay or timeScale on this branch\n\nAll 5 tests pass: tile-sharing, _buildDynamicWalkable correctness, moveTo detour,\ndeadlock escape (_failedMoveCount reset), and 120s live sim stability.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #157
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "content": "# AI Aquarium QA \u2014 Memory\n\n## Project\n- QA repo: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa.git at /home/gem/project\n- Source repo: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser.git (cloned per feature branch)\n- Tests: Playwright, `/home/gem/project/tests/`, runs against local dev servers\n\n## Source Repo Architecture (feature/frg-245 branch confirmed)\n- Static site, no package.json \u2014 serve with `python3 -m http.server PORT`\n- Phaser loaded from CDN: `cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/phaser@3.88.2`\n- `window._gameEngine` already exposed in `scene.js create()` \u2014 no route injection needed\n- Grid: 19 cols \u00d7 13 rows; interior walkable: col 1\u201317, row 2\u20139 (minus object tiles)\n- Character identifiers: use `c.label` (Maple/Sol/Nyx), NOT `c.id` (doesn't exist)\n\n## Actual Engine/Character API\n- `engine._buildDynamicWalkable(excludeChar)` \u2014 clones static grid, marks other chars blocked\n- `engine._findAdjacentWalkable(tx, ty, walkable?)` \u2014 first cardinal free tile\n- `engine._doWander(char)` \u2014 wander + deadlock escape logic\n- `engine.update(deltaMs)` \u2014 one sim tick (no manualTick, no timeScale)\n- `engine.objectEngine.getById(id)` \u2014 NOT `.get()`; IDs: stove,fridge,tea_set,kotatsu,tv,bookshelf,desk,bed,easel,bonsai\n- `char.moveTo(tx, ty, walkable?)` \u2014 A*, returns boolean; resets _failedMoveCount on success\n- `char._failedMoveCount` \u2014 deadlock counter; resets to 0 on success or escape\n- No `_moveCharWithCollision`, `_findFreeInteractTile`, `_retryTimer`, `_retryCount`, `_pendingTarget`, `manualTick`, `aiMode`, `snapshot`\n- Disable AI for tests: `engine.aiEngine = null`\n\n## Test Patterns\n- `page.evaluate()` runs synchronously (blocks rAF loop) \u2014 safe to call engine.update() multiple times\n- For 120s stability: call `engine.update(5000)` 24 times in one evaluate\n- Deadlock test: mock `char.moveTo = () => false`, call `_doWander()`, verify `_failedMoveCount === 0`\n- Port mapping by feature: frg-244\u21925173, frg-204\u21925174, frg-245\u21925175, frg-243\u21923000\n\n## Spec Files (tests/regression/)\n- `collision-avoidance.spec.js` \u2014 FRG-245, 5 tests, localhost:5175\n- `character-facing.spec.js` \u2014 FRG-244, localhost:5173\n- `walk-animation.spec.js` \u2014 FRG-204, localhost:5174\n- `contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js` \u2014 FRG-243, localhost:3000\n"
}

interior walkable: col 1\u201317, row 2\u20139 (minus object tiles)\n- Character identifiers: use `c.label` (Maple/Sol/Nyx), NOT `c.id` (doesn't exist)\n\n## Actual Engine/Character API\n- `engine._buildDynamicWalkable(excludeChar)` \u2014 clones static grid, marks other chars blocked\n- `engine._findAdjacentWalkable(tx, ty, walkable?)` \u2014 first cardinal free tile\n- `engine._doWander(char)` \u2014 wander + deadlock escape logic\n- `engine.update(deltaMs)` \u2014 one sim tick (no manualTick, no timeScale)\n- `engine.objectEngine.getById(id)` \u2014 NOT `.get()`; IDs: stove,fridge,tea_set,kotatsu,tv,bookshelf,desk,bed,easel,bonsai\n- `char.moveTo(tx, ty, walkable?)` \u2014 A*, returns boolean; resets _failedMoveCount on success\n- `char._failedMoveCount` \u2014 deadlock counter; resets to 0 on success or escape\n- No `_moveCharWithCollision`, `_findFreeInteractTile`, `_retryTimer`, `_retryCount`, `_pendingTarget`, `manualTick`, `aiMode`, `snapshot`\n- Disable AI for tests: `engine.aiEngine = null`\n\n## Test Patterns\n- `page.evaluate()` runs synchronously (blocks rAF loop) \u2014 safe to call engine.update() multiple times\n- For 120s stability: call `engine.update(5000)` 24 times in one evaluate\n- Deadlock test: mock `char.moveTo = () => false`, call `_doWander()`, verify `_failedMoveCount === 0`\n- Port mapping by feature: frg-244\u21925173, frg-204\u21925174, frg-245\u21925175, frg-243\u21923000\n\n## Spec Files (tests/regression/)\n- `collision-avoidance.spec.js` \u2014 FRG-245, 5 tests, localhost:5175\n- `character-facing.spec.js` \u2014 FRG-244, localhost:5173\n- `walk-animation.spec.js` \u2014 FRG-204, localhost:5174\n- `contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js` \u2014 FRG-243, localhost:3000\n"

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #159
## Status DONE ## What Changed - `tests/regression/collision-avoidance.spec.js` — Complete rewrite to use the actual Engine/Character API. Removed 7 tests using non-existent methods (`_moveCharWithCollision`, `_findFreeInteractTile`, `_retryTimer/_retryCount/_pendingTarget`, `manualTick`, `aiMode`, `snapshot`). Replaced with 5 correct tests covering the same behaviors via the real API. All 5 tests pass. ## Key Decisions - **Dropped route injection**: `window._gameEngine` is already unconditionally set in `scene.js create()` — no HTML patching needed - **`engine.update(deltaMs)` for stability test**: runs 120s of sim-time synchronously in one `page.evaluate()` (blocks rAF — no double-tick), replacing the non-existent `manualTick()` - **Deadlock escape via mocked `moveTo`**: overrides `char.moveTo = () => false` so all 15 wander attempts fail, triggering the escape path and resetting `_failedMoveCount` to 0 - **`c.label` not `c.id`**: characters have no `.id` property; `label` (Maple/Sol/Nyx) is the correct identifier - **MAP_COLS = 19** (was 16 in the old spec) - **Object ID `kotatsu` / row-2 corridor for path test**: avoided non-existent `cafe_espresso`; placed blocker at `(6,2)` on a straight-line path to force a verified detour through row 3 ## PR / Branch - Pushed directly to `main` on `chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa`
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "## Status\nDONE\n\n## What Changed\n- `tests/regression/collision-avoidance.spec.js` \u2014 Complete rewrite to use the actual Engine/Character API. Removed 7 tests using non-existent methods (`_moveCharWithCollision`, `_findFreeInteractTile`, `_retryTimer/_retryCount/_pendingTarget`, `manualTick`, `aiMode`, `snapshot`). Replaced with 5 correct tests covering the same behaviors via the real API. All 5 tests pass.\n\n## Key Decisions\n- **Dropped route injection**: `window._gameEngine` is already unconditionally set in `scene.js create()` \u2014 no HTML patching needed\n- **`engine.update(deltaMs)` for stability test**: runs 120s of sim-time synchronously in one `page.evaluate()` (blocks rAF \u2014 no double-tick), replacing the non-existent `manualTick()`\n- **Deadlock escape via mocked `moveTo`**: overrides `char.moveTo = () => false` so all 15 wander attempts fail, triggering the escape path and resetting `_failedMoveCount` to 0\n- **`c.label` not `c.id`**: characters have no `.id` property; `label` (Maple/Sol/Nyx) is the correct identifier\n- **MAP_COLS = 19** (was 16 in the old spec)\n- **Object ID `kotatsu` / row-2 corridor for path test**: avoided non-existent `cafe_espresso`; placed blocker at `(6,2)` on a straight-line path to force a verified detour through row 3\n\n## PR / Branch\n- Pushed directly to `main` on `chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa`"
  }
}
